news.title: FDA still focused on lettuce supplier as source of parasite, despite faulty test result
news.summary: The FDA said Monday it is still focused on lettuce from Taylor Farms as the source of a multistate outbreak of a diarrhea-causing parasite, despite a faulty test result.

news.title: STAT+: The next step of site-neutral, and a fiery PBM hearing
news.summary: Medicare proposes to reduce payments to hospitals for certain drugs and imaging services.

news.title: STAT+: A most-wanted fugitive appears to have been secretly working as a biotech executive
news.summary: A former doctor accused of sexual assault but on the run for 20 years appears to have been living a secret life as a biotech executive.

news.title: STAT+: Pharmalittle: We’re reading about a telehealth firm and patient safety, PBMs overcharging, and more
news.summary: Some former employees say telehealth company LifeMD has sought to maximize the volume of GLP-1 prescriptions at the expense of patient safety

news.title: HHS vaccine committee quietly gets new charter
news.summary: Lax clinical oversight for online GLP-1 prescriptions, proposed financial help for people who want to quit smoking, and more health news

news.title: STAT+: Bristol Myers Squibb becomes latest company to claim it’s building pharma’s largest NVIDIA AI supercomputer
news.summary: Bristol Myers Squibb is the third drugmaker in nine months to announce it is building the largest AI supercomputer in the life sciences industry.

news.title: Opinion: It’s time to treat pandemics as threats to national security
news.summary: “Pandemics should be viewed and combated as threats to national security,” write David Blumenthal and James A. Morone.

news.title: STAT+: State audit of Medicaid records points to methods used by PBMs to obscure drug costs
news.summary: An audit of state Medicaid records revealed how PBMs are using complicated tactics for handling prescription drug claims that ultimately overcharge taxpayers.

news.title: STAT+: Telemedicine company touted by Novo Nordisk stressed profits over patient safety, ex-workers say
news.summary: LifeMD, a popular telehealth company, has been accused by former employees of pressing clinicians to mete out prescriptions more rapidly without sufficient screening and follow-up.

news.title: Opinion: My husband’s suicide shows there’s something very wrong with the U.S. insurance industry
news.summary: “My love for Randy and my professional experience were not enough to save him,” a physician writes about her husband’s suicide.

news.title: DHS decouples duration of some visas from training program length
news.summary: About one in 10 physicians in a residency or fellowship program are sponsored under a J visa. Many training programs run longer than the new rule's four-year cap, thereby requiring students to apply for an Extension of Stay with the government.

news.title: Healthcare bankruptcy volume 'stabilizes' in H1, though more practices are falling under
news.summary: Quarterly bankruptcy filings appear to be leveling out after years of volatility, which "is surprising considering the on-the-ground pressures we see operators facing every day," a Gibbins Advisors principal said.

news.title: AI emerges as top healthcare IT investment globally, KLAS finds
news.summary: AI was named the top global healthcare IT investment priority among 57% of respondents, followed by EHRs and digitalization.

news.title: Healthcare affordability, fraud are key issues for voters heading into 2026 midterms
news.summary: Healthcare affordability is still the leading overall issue for voters heading into midterms, though another Trump administration talking point is gaining steam, according to a KFF survey report.

news.title: Clover Health reveals data breach in SEC filing
news.summary: Clover Health has disclosed a data breach in a new securities filing, though details on the incident are scarce.

news.title: Tempus buys out cancer genomics partner Personalis in $1.5 billion deal
news.summary: Tempus is boosting its cancer offerings with a deal to buy out Personalis three years after the pair first teamed up in oncology.

news.title: Why clinician-led IT is the future of care
news.summary: athenahealth explores why clinician-led innovation, AI and smarter workflows are redefining what health IT can deliver.

news.title: Close the Clinical Handoff Gap. Improve Post-Acute Outcomes.
news.summary: The biggest risk in care transitions isn't discharge—it's the handoff. Discover how plans can close the gap.

news.title: Op-ed: Accountability and access must coexist in hospice care
news.summary: Oversight must be thoughtful and data-driven to distinguish between organizations committed to long-term quality and those exploiting the system, write Compassus' CEO and Providence's chief of community services.

news.title: UnitedHealthcare exec says 'ineffective' No Surprises Act IDR process demands reform
news.summary: Industry giant UnitedHealthcare is adding its voice to the chorus of insurers criticizing the current state of the No Surprises Act's independent dispute resolution process.

news.title: Weekly Rundown: Novant Health rolls out new virtual primary care model; GW RhythmX launches Pulse on Microsoft Marketplace
news.summary: Stay up to date on the latest in health tech, digital health and health AI news with this weekly brief.

news.title: Senate strikes down Dems move to overturn CMS’ WISeR AI prior auth pilot
news.summary: Democratic lawmakers in the Senate and House of Representatives introduced resolutions back in May to stop the WISeR model that introduces AI-driven prior authorization to traditional Medicare.

news.title: Scribe aims for $96M IPO to fund clinical push for lipid-lowering genetic meds
news.summary: Scribe Therapeutics has put its IPO plans down on paper, showing the genetic medicine company hopes to raise $96 million through its public listing.

news.title: Kolon’s cell-based gene therapy fails phase 3 osteoarthritis trial
news.summary: A phase 3 trial of Kolon TissueGene’s cell-based gene therapy has missed its co-primary endpoints. With a second late-stage study scheduled to report data in October, the biotech is holding off on determining the next steps for the osteoarthritis candidate.

news.title: De-Risking a Rare Disease Launch Starts with Precision
news.summary: Why traditional models fall short in rare disease commercialization—and what works instead.

news.title: Why early de-risking is redefining the path to IND
news.summary: Lonza explains why early de-risking has become a strategic imperative for biotech teams working to reach IND with confidence.

news.title: Geopolitics ‘chills’ British biotech IPO plans, despite $2B in Q2 VC funds
news.summary: Geopolitical uncertainty has continued to “chill” IPO sentiment among British biotechs, an industry leader has told Fierce, even as the sector saw second-quarter venture capital funding reach a five-year high.

news.title: ‘A rising tide scenario’: Why other Big Pharmas could follow Lilly into psychedelics
news.summary: After Eli Lilly dived into the psychedelics space with a $2.8 billion upfront acquisition of AtaiBeckley last week, industry experts told Fierce that the deal would give the space added momentum.

news.title: Latigo preps IPO to fund non-opioid pain drug rival to Vertex’s Journax
news.summary: Latigo Biotherapeutics is preparing to go public in order to bankroll the development of its clinical-stage pipeline of non-opioid pain management drugs.

papers.title: Benchmarking Sensor Robustness in Plasma Diagnostic Models: A Systematic Evaluation on TokaMark
papers.summary: Plasma diagnostic models for tokamak fusion devices are almost universally evaluated on clean, complete sensor data. In practice, fusion diagnostics fail regularly: acquisition systems start late, individual sensors die, and signal dropouts cluster precisely when a plasma disruption is approaching. We present the first systematic robustness benchmark for plasma diagnostic ML using the TokaMark dataset of 11,573 MAST shots, evaluating XGBoost, LSTM, Transformer, and the TokaMark CNN baseline across six physically-grounded failure scenarios and three imputation strategies. We introduce the Robus

papers.title: Cura 1T: Specialized Model for Agentic Healthcare
papers.summary: Healthcare spans high-stakes communication, expert reasoning, and workflow execution, yet specialized LLMs that cover these use cases together remain limited. A healthcare model must handle patient consultation, clinical reasoning over text and images, interactive diagnosis, and electronic health record (EHR) tool use. These capabilities fail in different ways, and a narrow update for one task can degrade another. We present Cura 1T, a healthcare-specialized LLM trained through a human-gated self-evolution loop.
